title: Rulingia dasyphylla
intro: Rulingia dasyphylla, commonly known as Rulingia, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Goodeniaceae. It has been traditionally used in Western Australia by the Indigenous people to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and coughs, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Rulingia dasyphylla include diterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, and in some cases, it is also used to make a cold infusion or a tincture, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues and relieve pain.
taxonomy: Rulingia dasyphylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae. It is native to Western Australia. The plant is also known as the 'bushy curly-stem' or 'curly stem'. Rulingia dasyphylla is a shrub that grows up to 2 meters tall. The plant is commonly found in dry to wet soils, often in woodland and along roadsides.
